message:
chore(beads): sync issues.jsonl

Routine sync. Append-only beads metadata; no historical row deletions.

Untracked artifacts test_env_read and test_guard intentionally not
staged.

files.patch:
@@ -1771,10 +1771,10 @@
 {"id":"br-bn0vb.11","title":"ATC Seam 4.3: outcome resolution on ack-overdue sweep","description":"# ATC Seam 4.3: Ack-overdue sweep

## Preconditions

- br-bn0vb.3 + br-bn0vb.6 + br-bn0vb.9 (Seam 4.1) closed

## Scope

For MessageSent experiences with ack_required=true, after the attribution window passes without an Acknowledge:
- Sweep queries open rows older than attribution_window
- Resolves as AckOverdue
- Emits an operator-observable event (appears in the views/ack-overdue resource already)

## Implementation

Piggyback on ATC tick. Calls query_open_experiences + resolve_experience loop.

## Tests

- Integration: send with ack_required + no ack within window → sweep resolves AckOverdue
- Integration: send without ack_required → not resolved by this sweep

## Definition of done

- Sweep wired
- Tests pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:22:40.223729780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:18.369020060Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","outcome-resolution","seam-4","sweep"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.11","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:22:40.223729780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.11","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:17.638737568Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.11","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:18.368363581Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-bn0vb.12","title":"ATC Seam 5.1: am robot atc reads live snapshot (not lightweight lock tracker)","description":"# ATC Seam 5.1: Robot ATC reads live snapshot

## Preconditions

- br-bn0vb.6 + br-bn0vb.7 (seam 3 wiring complete) so snapshot has content to show
- br-bn0vb.9 + br-bn0vb.10 (seam 4 resolution) so resolved outcomes show up in snapshot

## Scope

Currently, `am robot atc` reports availability + lock tracking only (README L650: "intentionally lightweight today"). Upgrade it to read `atc_operator_snapshot()` / `atc_summary()` directly from the running server.

## Implementation outline

1. In `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/robot.rs`, the `atc` subcommand currently returns a `ToolingLocksData` or similar. Change it to:
   - Make an HTTP/MCP call to the server's `atc_operator_snapshot()` method (or via a resource like `resource://atc/snapshot`)
   - Format per `--format` arg (toon/json/md)
   - Include: total experiences, open/resolved/censored/expired counts, recent decisions, policy version, safe-mode status, fairness metrics

2. If the server is not running, fall back to reading the DB directly (read-only query on atc_experiences + rollups). Document this fallback in the subcommand help.

3. Add flags: `--since`, `--stratum`, `--summary-only`, `--limit`

## Tests

- Integration: server running + 10 experiences written + resolved → `am robot atc --format json` returns expected shape with non-zero counts
- Integration: server not running → fallback to DB read, still returns snapshot
- Integration: empty DB → snapshot shows zero counts (not an error)

## Definition of done

- Subcommand reads live state
- --format supported across toon/json/md
- Help text accurate
- README L650 "intentionally lightweight" note can be removed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:22:40.740626491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:19.651735616Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","robot","seam-5"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.12","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:22:40.740626491Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.12","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:19.102671321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.12","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:19.651206855Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-bn0vb.13","title":"ATC Seam 5.2: TUI Atc screen + System Health consume snapshot only","description":"# ATC Seam 5.2: TUI surfaces consume ATC snapshot

## Preconditions

- br-bn0vb.12 (Seam 5.1 robot) closed (shares the snapshot shape)

## Scope

Two TUI surfaces:

### Atc screen (`tui_screens/atc.rs`)
Replace any placeholder content with sections driven from `atc_operator_snapshot()`:
- Top strip: availability + policy version + safe-mode status + last-tick timestamp
- Middle: recent decisions table (last N, with: timestamp, decision, action, outcome-if-resolved)
- Lower: fairness metrics, identifiability debt, risk budget health
- Footer: keybindings for drill-in (e.g., `d` for decision detail, `r` for retention report)

### System Health screen (`tui_screens/system_health.rs`)
Add an ATC section showing ATC tick health:
- Last tick time, tick latency p95
- Rollup refresh status
- Retention policy compliance

## Constraints

- Both surfaces are read-only (per Implementation Map: "consumers of shared snapshot, not alternate sources of truth")
- No direct DB reads from TUI; go through the ATC snapshot API

## Tests

- Unit: render each section with a known snapshot payload
- Integration: run server, observe TUI reflecting live state as workload runs

## Definition of done

- Atc screen shows live data, not placeholder
- System Health has ATC section
- Tests pass","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:22:41.246878656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:22:41.246878656Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","seam-5","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.13","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:22:41.246878656Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-bn0vb.14","title":"ATC Seam 6.1: E2E test — full learning event from dispatch to resolved snapshot","description":"# ATC Seam 6.1: End-to-end learning event test

## Preconditions

- All Seam 1-5 closed
- br-bn0vb.12 + br-bn0vb.13 (operator surfaces live) closed

## Scope

Add `tests/e2e/test_atc_learning_loop.sh` (or .rs) that demonstrates a complete learning event:

1. Start a fresh server with a clean DB
2. Register two agents (A, B) in a test project
3. A sends an ack_required message to B
4. Assert: one new row in atc_experiences with event_kind=MessageSent, state=OPEN
5. B fetches inbox
6. Assert: one row with event_kind=MessageReceived, state=OPEN
7. B acknowledges
8. Assert: the MessageSent row transitions to RESOLVED with label=Acknowledged
9. `am robot atc --format json` shows 2 total experiences, 1 resolved, 1 open (the Receive side)
10. Teardown

## Additional scenarios to cover in the same test

- Reservation grant → release → resolved as CleanRelease
- Reservation grant → conflict → resolved as Conflicted
- Message with ack_required but no ack + wait for sweep → resolved as AckOverdue
- Retention compaction runs during test and does NOT delete recent rows

## Definition of done

- Test passes locally and in CI
- Failure modes produce actionable logs
- Test runs in < 60 seconds","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:23:15.723020029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:22.838443461Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","e2e","seam-6","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.14","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:23:15.723020029Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.14","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:22.110139875Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.14","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:22.837851392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.14","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:20.804362600Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.14","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:21.349822474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-bn0vb.15","title":"ATC Seam 6.2: perf regression guard — send_message p95 < 5% overhead","description":"# ATC Seam 6.2: Perf regression guard

## Preconditions

- br-bn0vb.6-11 (seam 3+4 wiring) closed

## Scope

Capture a pre-wiring baseline for `send_message` p50/p95/p99 latency, then verify post-wiring latency is within 5%.

## Deliverable

- Add a benchmark to `am bench` catalog: `mail_send_with_atc` and `mail_send_no_atc` (latter controlled by an env var like ATC_LEARNING_DISABLED=1)
- Baseline numbers captured in `benches/BUDGETS.md`
- CI job or local bench script that flags > 5% regression

## Considerations

- The reality-check found send_message p95 at 27.1ms (2026-02-09 baseline). 5% headroom = ~1.4ms.
- If the direct insert path exceeds this headroom, use the deferred-write channel pattern from Seam 3.1.
- Do NOT relax the budget to accommodate ATC. Optimization belongs in Seam 2.1 if insert is slow.

## Definition of done

- Baseline captured
- Post-wiring numbers within 5% of baseline (< ~1.4ms absolute)
- BUDGETS.md updated","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:23:16.272557369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:24.136625853Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","perf","seam-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.15","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:23:16.272557369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.15","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:24.135999150Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.15","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:23.574387847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-bn0vb.16","title":"ATC Seam 6.3: retention + compaction soak (7-day simulated workload)","description":"# ATC Seam 6.3: Retention soak test

## Preconditions

- br-bn0vb.5 (retention_compact) closed
- Full seam 3-5 wiring in place

## Scope

Simulate a 7-day workload with virtual time (asupersync LabRuntime supports this) and verify:
- Experience rows older than retention TTL are compacted
- Rollup rows are preserved per policy
- DB file size stays bounded (within a configurable ceiling)
- No data loss for recent rows during compaction
- Compaction perf stays within budget (< 100ms per tick for 100K rows)

## Implementation

Use asupersync `LabRuntime` deterministic virtual-time harness. Inject synthetic workload (e.g., 1000 events/hour for 7 days = 168K events). Compaction runs hourly. After day 7, DB should contain only events within the last `retain_recent` window (e.g., last 7 days) + all rollups.

## Definition of done

- Test passes
- Bounded-growth property verified
- Compaction perf within budget","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:23:16.840608953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:24.895407880Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","retention","seam-6","soak"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.16","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:23:16.840608953Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.16","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:24.894779733Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-bn0vb.17","title":"ATC Seam 7: remove 'architecture-phase' disclaimer from README once learning loop validated","description":"# ATC Seam 7: Disclaimer removal

## Preconditions

- br-bn0vb.14 + br-bn0vb.15 + br-bn0vb.16 (Seam 6 all validated) closed

## Scope

Once the learning loop is closed and validated, remove the 'architecture-phase' callout added under Epic 1 (br-o217s.6) from the README.

## Coordination

- Cross-reference br-o217s.6 (the bead that ADDS the disclaimer)
- This bead is the paired REMOVER — only executable after Seam 6 validation

## Edits

- Remove the `> **Status... architecture-phase, not learning-phase...**` callout from README ATC section
- Update the ATC Learning Implementation Map section's "recommended implementation order" to reflect that steps 1-5 are done; leave step 6 (verification) checked off
- Update CHANGELOG.md with "ATC learning loop operational" entry

## Verification

```bash
rg -nF 'architecture-phase' README.md   # 0
rg -nF 'atc_note_' crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs   # >= 5 (one per hot-path event kind)
```

## Definition of done

- Disclaimer gone
- CHANGELOG updated

 {"id":"br-bn0vb.2","title":"ATC Seam 2.1: DB insert_experience() append API","description":"# ATC Seam 2.1: DB append API for atc_experiences

## Preconditions

- br-bn0vb.1 (Seam 1) closed

## Scope

Add to `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/` a dedicated module `atc_queries.rs` (or similar) containing an atomic append API for `atc_experiences` rows.

## API signature (draft)

```rust
pub async fn insert_experience(
    cx: &Cx,
    pool: &DbPool,
    row: ExperienceRow,
) -> Result<ExperienceId, DbError> { ... }
```

## Requirements

- Single-statement INSERT, no join-like work
- Idempotent by experience_id (INSERT OR IGNORE, or ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
- Returns the assigned experience_id
- Use `BEGIN IMMEDIATE` (per frankensqlite notes in MEMORY.md — `BEGIN CONCURRENT` unsafe outside FrankenConnection)
- Bounded latency: < 500µs p95 on reference hardware
- Emits structured tracing span: `atc.db.insert_experience { experience_id, feature_vector_size, ... }`
- Respects `micros_to_naive`/`naive_to_micros` timestamp convention

## Tests required

- Unit: round-trip a row, verify all fields preserved
- Unit: duplicate insert is no-op (idempotent)
- Unit: concurrent inserts from N tasks do not corrupt or lose rows (stress the pool)
- Perf: 1000 inserts in tight loop, measure p50/p95/p99

## Considerations

- Schema reference: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-db/src/schema.rs` v16 `atc_experiences` definition
- Respect existing pool hardening (WAL mode, 60s busy_timeout)
- Do NOT write to Git archive from this path — ATC rows are SQLite-only per README Implementation Map cold/warm/hot boundary

## Definition of done

- API implemented + tested
- Perf within budget
- No regression on send_message p95 when this API is called (verified via isolated microbench)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:21:18.210129211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:11.265727005Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","db","seam-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.2","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:21:18.210129211Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.2","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:11.265005374Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-bn0vb.3","title":"ATC Seam 2.2: DB resolve_experience() with outcome labels + censor reasons","description":"# ATC Seam 2.2: DB resolve API

## Preconditions

- br-bn0vb.2 (insert API) closed

## Scope

Add `resolve_experience(experience_id, outcome, censor_reason?, attribution_window?)` to `atc_queries.rs`. Transitions a row from OPEN → RESOLVED (or CENSORED or EXPIRED) exactly once.

## API signature (draft)

```rust
pub async fn resolve_experience(
    cx: &Cx,
    pool: &DbPool,
    experience_id: ExperienceId,
    resolution: Resolution,
) -> Result<ResolveOutcome, DbError> { ... }

pub enum Resolution {
    Resolved { label: OutcomeLabel, attribution_window_micros: i64 },
    Censored { reason: CensorReason },
    Expired,
}

pub enum ResolveOutcome {
    Applied,          // row transitioned
    AlreadyResolved,  // idempotent no-op
    NotFound,         // experience_id doesn't exist
}
```

## Requirements

- Idempotent: calling resolve twice with same outcome is a no-op, returns AlreadyResolved
- Calling resolve with different outcomes after the first must be rejected (not silently overwritten) — this is a critical invariant for learning integrity
- Uses atc_labeling.rs for OutcomeLabel values; atc_labeling defines 7 action-family rules and 8 censor reasons per memory log 2026-03-18
- Atomic within a single transaction
- Emits structured tracing span with transition details
- Updates rollup counters (but rollup logic can live in Seam 2.3 and be called from this function)

## Tests

- Unit: happy path OPEN → RESOLVED
- Unit: idempotent double-resolve with same outcome
- Unit: double-resolve with DIFFERENT outcome → error (preserves learning integrity)
- Unit: resolve non-existent id → NotFound
- Property: any sequence of resolve calls respects the monotonicity invariant (no going backward)

## Definition of done

- API implemented + tested
- Integration with atc_labeling's OutcomeLabel enum
- Rollup invocation stubbed or integrated (depends on Seam 2.3)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:21:18.631154291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:21:18.631154291Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","db","seam-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.3","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:21:18.631154291Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-bn0vb.4","title":"ATC Seam 2.3: rollup maintenance + stratum aggregation","description":"# ATC Seam 2.3: Rollup aggregation

## Preconditions

- br-bn0vb.2 (insert) closed
- br-bn0vb.3 (resolve) closed

## Scope

Maintain the `atc_experience_rollups` v16 table. Rollups are pre-aggregated summaries per (stratum, time_bucket) so ATC policy engine and operator surfaces can read fast.

## Strategy

Two possible designs:

### Option 1: Inline-update rollups on every insert/resolve
Simple, keeps rollups real-time, but adds cost to hot path.

### Option 2: Batch-recompute rollups on a periodic tick (e.g., every 5 min)
Adds latency to rollups but keeps insert/resolve fast. Still real-time enough for learning.

Recommended: **Option 2** per the README Implementation Map "append-friendly hot path, free of write amplification".

## API signature

```rust
pub async fn refresh_rollups(
    cx: &Cx,
    pool: &DbPool,
    now_micros: i64,
    lookback_micros: i64,
) -> Result<RollupSummary, DbError> { ... }
```

## Requirements

- Scan `atc_experiences` for rows updated in the last `lookback` window
- Compute per-stratum counts (OPEN/RESOLVED/CENSORED/EXPIRED by outcome label and action family)
- UPSERT into `atc_experience_rollups`
- Emit structured span
- Bounded runtime: < 100ms for 100K experience rows

## Tick integration

The ATC tick loop in `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/atc.rs` already runs at a cadence (memory log mentions DecisionCore.alpha matching replay tick cadence). Piggyback rollup refresh on that tick.

## Tests

- Unit: single experience → rollup row with count=1
- Unit: N experiences, varied stratums → correct per-stratum counts
- Unit: re-running refresh on same data is idempotent (no double-count)
- Perf: 100K rows rolled up in < 100ms

## Definition of done

- refresh_rollups() implemented + tested
- Integrated into ATC tick
- Rollup table correctly populated after test workload","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:21:19.064562394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:12.106941011Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atc","db","seam-2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.4","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:21:19.064562394Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-bn0vb.4","depends_on_id":"br-bn0vb.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:12.106267520Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-il53l.4","title":"SHIP: implement POST /mail/ws-input handler for browser input forwarding","description":"# SHIP-branch task: Implement /mail/ws-input POST handler

## Preconditions

- br-il53l.1 (decision) resolved to SHIP
- br-il53l.2 (spec) closed

## Scope

Implement `POST /mail/ws-input` per the spec. Receives browser Input/Resize events, forwards them to the live TUI, returns the post-apply sequence number.

## Implementation outline

1. Parse JSON body, validate against `{type, data}` schema
2. Bearer auth
3. Resolve target TUI instance (there is a single server-side TUI per process; if multiple operators are connected, decide on event semantics — probably last-writer-wins with audit in a log)
4. Dispatch the Input or Resize event into the TUI event loop
5. Wait for the TUI to process and publish the next state sequence
6. Respond with `{seq, status: "applied"}` or `{seq, status: "rejected", reason}`
7. Audit log: every input event recorded with (timestamp, bearer_token_hash, event_type, key_or_resize, source_ip) so multi-operator conflicts are debuggable

## Security considerations

- CSRF: require bearer token in `Authorization` header, not cookies, for all ws-input requests
- Rate limit: bound input rate per bearer token (e.g., 50 events/sec) to prevent DoS
- No command-injection: key events dispatched as typed keycodes, never as command strings
- No privilege escalation: the TUI commands a browser operator can send are the same as what the physical terminal user can do — do NOT allow ws-input to execute anything the TUI itself wouldn't expose

## Test plan

- Unit: input schema validation edge cases
- Unit: rate limit enforcement
- Integration: send Input event, verify TUI state changes in next snapshot
- Security: attempt to inject a shell command via key events — must fail because TUI doesn't eval them

## Non-goals

- Not designing multi-operator concurrency beyond last-writer-wins + audit (that's a later concern if multi-op is ever expected)

## Definition of done

- Handler compiles, tests pass
- Manual curl test per README L776-779 returns correct JSON
- Audit log wired and observable","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:08.652537609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:08.652537609Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["server","ship-branch","wasm","web-ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-il53l.4","depends_on_id":"br-il53l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:08.652537609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-il53l.5","title":"SHIP: build WASM canvas/SVG renderer that reproduces the 16-screen TUI","description":"# SHIP-branch task: WASM renderer for the 16-screen TUI

## Preconditions

- br-il53l.1 (decision) resolved to SHIP
- br-il53l.2 (spec) closed
- br-il53l.3 (GET /ws-state) working (renderer needs something to consume)

## Scope

Flesh out `crates/mcp-agent-mail-wasm/src/` into a functional renderer. Current state is a 363-line skeleton + 851-line state-store without a paired draw loop. The renderer should:

1. Consume the state-delta stream from `GET /mail/ws-state`
2. Apply deltas to an internal state store
3. Render each of the 16 TUI screens to an HTML canvas (or SVG — choose one; canvas likely simpler for grid rendering)
4. Capture keyboard + resize events and POST them to `/mail/ws-input`
5. Handle auth token pickup from URL `?token=...` per README L809

## Implementation outline

- Decide rendering target: canvas 2D (likely) vs SVG vs WebGL. Canvas 2D fits the TUI grid model best.
- Implement glyph grid: N cols × M rows, each cell is (char, fg_color, bg_color, attrs). Matches frankentui cell model.
- Implement theme support: at minimum Cyberpunk Aurora + High Contrast (matches TUI themes)
- Implement the 16 screen renderers as dispatched handlers keyed on `state.active_screen`
- Handle special rendering: sparklines, Braille heatmaps, markdown-rendered thread bodies, syntax-highlighted code blocks
- Implement keyboard capture + forwarding to POST /ws-input
- Implement resize observer → Resize event

## Test plan

- Visual regression: golden screenshots of each of 16 screens, compared via pixel diff
- E2E: real browser (headless Chromium via playwright or similar) loads /web-dashboard, shows correct content
- Performance: render loop at 30 FPS target on a modest machine (tracking: render time per frame)

## Considerations

- This is a large task. Consider breaking into sub-tasks per screen if estimate runs too large. For now, single bead with a clear DoD.
- Do NOT re-implement the TUI's business logic on the client. The renderer is strictly a viewer; all decisions happen server-side.
- Fallback behavior: if state is older than retention, show a "Reconnecting..." overlay and re-fetch full snapshot.

## Definition of done

- All 16 screens render correctly from live server state
- Keyboard input roundtrip works (key press in browser → TUI responds → new state rendered)
- Theme switching works
- Playwright E2E test green

## Considerations for estimate

If this task exceeds 40 hours, split into: "renderer-core (6 common screens) + keyboard" / "renderer-rest (10 remaining screens)" / "renderer-polish (themes, sparklines, heatmaps)".","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:59.827969878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:02.703373895Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["renderer","ship-branch","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-il53l.5","depends_on_id":"br-il53l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:18:59.827969878Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-il53l.5","depends_on_id":"br-il53l.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:02.702959318Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-il53l.6","title":"SHIP: /web-dashboard bootstrap HTML + WASM module load + auth token pickup","description":"# SHIP-branch task: /web-dashboard HTML bootstrap

## Preconditions

- br-il53l.1 (decision) resolved to SHIP
- br-il53l.5 (renderer) deliverable (module must be loadable)

## Scope

Implement `/web-dashboard`, `/web-dashboard/state`, `/web-dashboard/input` as described in README L784-817. Key requirements:
- Serve an HTML page that loads the compiled WASM module
- Pick up auth token from `?token=<HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN>` query parameter and reuse it for polling
- Dispatch to `live` / `warming` / `inactive` mode based on whether a live terminal TUI is attached
- In `inactive` mode, show passive telemetry instead of pretending the mirror works (already partially implemented per README L800)

## Implementation outline

1. Template: `crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/templates/web_dashboard.html` (minijinja). Boot script loads WASM + starts polling.
2. Server handler in `tui_web_dashboard.rs`: renders the template, passes in auth token status + mode
3. Auth handling:
   - HTML request unauthorized → remediation page (already per README L812)
   - JSON state/input unauthorized → JSON 401 (already per README L812)
4. Mode detection: query the TUI subsystem for "is live TUI attached?" and "has first frame been captured?"
5. Polling in browser: reuse query token for Authorization header on every poll

## Test plan

- Unit: mode detection returns correct state for live/warming/inactive
- Integration: request /web-dashboard with valid token → 200 + HTML with embedded boot script; without token → remediation page
- E2E: headless browser loads page, WASM boots, first frame rendered

## Definition of done

- /web-dashboard and /web-dashboard/state and /web-dashboard/input all wired
- Auth token handling works per README L809
- Mode dispatch works (verified manually by running `serve --no-tui` vs `serve` and observing the mode badge)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:19:00.259749091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:19:00.259749091Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ship-branch","wasm","web-ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-il53l.6","depends_on_id":"br-il53l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:19:00.259749091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-il53l.7","title":"SHIP: E2E headless browser test — /web-dashboard full roundtrip","description":"# SHIP-branch task: Headless browser E2E test

## Preconditions

- br-il53l.1 (decision) resolved to SHIP
- br-il53l.3-6 all closed (contract + renderer + bootstrap all working)

## Scope

Add an E2E test under `tests/e2e/test_web_dashboard.sh` (or `tests/e2e/test_web_dashboard.rs`) that:

1. Boots `mcp-agent-mail serve` with TUI
2. Launches headless Chromium (playwright or chromedriver)
3. Navigates to `http://127.0.0.1:8765/web-dashboard?token=<test-token>`
4. Verifies the page loads (HTTP 200, expected HTML markers)
5. Waits for WASM module to boot + first frame to render
6. Verifies rendered canvas contains expected content (e.g., "Dashboard" heading visible)
7. Sends a simulated keypress (e.g., `2` to switch to Messages screen)
8. Verifies Messages screen is now rendered
9. Sends a Resize event, verifies state snapshot reflects new size
10. Tears down

## Test harness considerations

- If playwright isn't already a test dep, use chromedriver via a minimal shell driver. Prefer whichever keeps dep weight low.
- This test is allowed to run headless-only in CI; dev machines can run headed for debugging.
- Add to CI workflow as an optional/gated job if the browser install is heavy.

## Non-goals

- Not testing every visual detail of every screen (covered by visual regression in br-il53l.5)
- Not load testing

## Definition of done

- Test script + harness committed
- Test passes on local dev + CI
- Failure modes produce actionable logs (screenshots on failure, console output captured)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:19:00.699875729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:03.696640366Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","ship-branch","testing","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-il53l.7","depends_on_id":"br-il53l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:19:00.699875729Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-il53l.7","depends_on_id":"br-il53l.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:03.276286542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-il53l.7","depends_on_id":"br-il53l.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:03.696060550Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"br-il53l.8","title":"SHIP: security review of /mail/ws-state, /mail/ws-input, /web-dashboard","description":"# SHIP-branch task: Security review for browser-dashboard surface

## Preconditions

- br-il53l.3-6 all implemented

## Scope

Before promoting /web-dashboard to a documented feature, a security review must:

1. **Bearer auth coverage**: every endpoint (`/mail/ws-state`, `/mail/ws-input`, `/web-dashboard`, `/web-dashboard/state`, `/web-dashboard/input`) rejects unauthenticated requests per the documented policy (html → remediation page, json → 401)
2. **Query-token leak risk**: `?token=<HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN>` ends up in Referer headers, browser history, server access logs. Document the tradeoff. Recommend a short-lived session token exchange instead of long-lived bearer.
3. **XSS**: any state payload that contains user-submitted content (message subjects, bodies, agent names) is rendered only through the canvas glyph grid — which is XSS-safe by construction — BUT, also audit the HTML bootstrap template for unescaped injection points.
4. **CSRF**: POST /mail/ws-input requires bearer in `Authorization` header, not cookies. Confirm this is enforced and that the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header is set correctly (e.g., only same-origin or explicit allow-list).
5. **Rate limits**: polling-side and input-side rate limits in place, documented, and tested.
6. **Audit log**: /mail/ws-input events are logged with enough detail for post-hoc review, but without logging bearer tokens themselves (log the hash/kid, not the token).
7. **Privilege boundary**: browser operator can only do what the physical TUI operator can do — no additional commands, no admin-mode.
8. **Headers**: CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options reviewed.

## Deliverable

`docs/SPEC-web-dashboard-security.md` + code fixes for any findings.

## Definition of done

- Security spec written
- All findings either fixed or filed as follow-up beads with severity
- Repo owner signs off on the review","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:19:01.170233741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:05.445289959Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["security","ship-branch","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-il53l.8","depends_on_id":"br-il53l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:19:01.170233741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-il53l.8","depends_on_id":"br-il53l.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:04.449753115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-il53l.8","depends_on_id":"br-il53l.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:04.855529348Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-il53l.8","depends_on_id":"br-il53l.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T01:26:05.444546016Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

## Preconditions

## Scope

Before promoting /web-dashboard to a documented feature, a security review must:

1. **Bearer auth coverage**: every endpoint (`/mail/ws-state`, `/mail/ws-input`, `/web-dashboard`, `/web-dashboard/state`, `/web-dashboard/input`) rejects unauthenticated requests per the documented policy (html → remediation page, json → 401)
2. **Query-token leak risk**: `?token=<HTTP_BEARER_TOKEN>` ends up in Referer headers, browser history, server access logs. Document the tradeoff. Recommend a short-lived session token exchange instead of long-lived bearer.
3. **XSS**: any state payload that contains user-submitted content (message subjects, bodies, agent names) is rendered only through the canvas glyph grid — which is XSS-safe by construction — BUT, also audit the HTML bootstrap template for unescaped injection points.
4. **CSRF**: POST /mail/ws-input requires bearer in `Authorization` header, not cookies. Confirm this is enforced and that the `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header is set correctly (e.g., only same-origin or explicit allow-list).
5. **Rate limits**: polling-side and input-side rate limits in place, documented, and tested.
6. **Audit log**: /mail/ws-input events are logged with enough detail for post-hoc review, but without logging bearer tokens themselves (log the hash/kid, not the token).
7. **Privilege boundary**: browser operator can only do what the physical TUI operator can do — no additional commands, no admin-mode.
8. **Headers**: CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options reviewed.

 {"id":"br-is0pn","title":"ATC Track 3: Conflict Detector — Wait-For Graph + Tarjan SCC","description":"# Track 3: Conflict Detector (Wait-For Graph + Tarjan SCC)

## Graveyard Source: Tarjan's SCC (graph algorithms), Wait-For Graphs (DB theory)

## What
Maintains a real-time resource dependency graph across all agents and file
reservations. Detects two distinct classes of problems:

1. **Hard conflicts (deadlocks)**: actual overlapping exclusive reservations
   creating circular wait chains. Only hard conflicts can deadlock.
2. **Soft conflicts (advisories)**: interest signals suggesting two agents are
   converging on the same files without proper coordination.

This distinction is critical: deadlock detection (Tarjan SCC) only runs on
the hard conflict graph. Soft conflicts trigger lightweight advisories.

## Why a Graph, Not Pairwise Checks
Pairwise conflict detection is O(n²) agents and misses transitive deadlocks
(A→B→C→A). A directed graph with SCC detection is:
- O(V+E) per check via Tarjan's algorithm
- Catches cycles of any length
- Only runs on reservation change events (not every tick)
- Provides the exact cycle for targeted resolution

## Critical Design Considerations

### Multi-Project Scoping
Reservations are scoped per-project. An agent holding src/lib.rs in project A
does NOT conflict with an agent holding src/lib.rs in project B. The conflict
graph must partition by project_key:
```rust
struct ConflictGraph {
    per_project: HashMap<String, ProjectConflictGraph>,
}
```

### Self-Exclusion
The ATC agent ("AirTrafficControl") must be excluded from conflict tracking.

### Advisory Rate Limiting
Per-agent cooldown (default: 1 advisory per agent per 5 minutes).
```rust
struct AdvisoryThrottle {
    last_advisory: HashMap<AgentId, i64>,
    cooldown_micros: i64, // default: 300_000_000 (5 min)
}
```

### Existing Pattern Matching Infrastructure
Reuse fnmatch overlap logic from crates/mcp-agent-mail-guard/src/lib.rs.

## Data Model

### Hard Conflict Graph (Per-Project)
Only includes edges where BOTH agents hold active, overlapping, exclusive
reservations. These are the only edges that can form deadlock cycles.

```rust
struct HardEdge {
    holder: AgentId,
    blocked: AgentId,
    contested_patterns: Vec<String>,
    since: i64,
}

struct ProjectConflictGraph {
    project_key: String,
    /// Hard edges: overlapping exclusive reservations
    hard_edges: HashMap<AgentId, Vec<HardEdge>>,
    /// Soft edges: interest signals (for advisories only, NOT deadlock detection)
    soft_interests: HashMap<AgentId, HashMap<String, InterestSignal>>,
    /// All active reservations indexed by pattern
    reservations_by_pattern: HashMap<String, Vec<(AgentId, ReservationInfo)>>,
    generation: u64,
}
```

### Hard Conflict Detection (Event-Driven, Only on Reservation Changes)
On every reservation_created/released/renewed event:
1. Identify the project scope
2. Update reservations_by_pattern for that project
3. For each pattern in the new reservation, check for overlapping EXCLUSIVE
   reservations held by other agents (using fnmatch overlap from guard crate)
4. If exclusive overlap found, add HardEdge(s) to the graph
5. Run Tarjan's SCC on the hard_edges graph only
6. Any SCC with |V| > 1 is a deadlock cycle

### Soft Conflict Detection (Event-Driven, On Messages)
On message events, extract file patterns from the subject line (lightweight
pattern matching, NOT full body parsing):
1. Look for patterns like "src/*.rs", "lib.rs", "Cargo.toml" in subject
2. Record as InterestSignal for the sending agent
3. If two agents have recent interest in overlapping patterns and neither has
   a reservation → send advisory (respecting rate limit)

Soft conflicts NEVER trigger Tarjan SCC or deadlock resolution. They're
purely informational advisories.

### Tarjan's SCC Implementation (Hard Edges Only)
```rust
fn find_deadlock_cycles(graph: &ProjectConflictGraph) -> Vec<Vec<AgentId>> {
    // Standard Tarjan's algorithm on hard_edges only:
    // - DFS with index/lowlink tracking
    // - Stack-based SCC collection
    // - Return only SCCs with |V| > 1 (true cycles)
    // O(V+E) time, O(V) space
}
```

### Deadlock Resolution Protocol
When a cycle is detected:
1. Score each agent in the cycle:
   ```
   score = reservation_age_micros * 0.4
         + time_since_last_message * 0.3
         + inverse_task_priority * 0.3
   ```
2. Feed into the conflict DecisionCore (Track 1) with loss matrix
3. If action = advisory_message: send to the lowest-scoring agent
   (respecting rate limit):
   ```
   [ATC] Deadlock detected in {project}. You hold {patterns} needed by
   {other_agent}. Consider releasing to unblock the cycle.
   ```
4. If action = force_reservation: auto-release the lowest-scorer's oldest
   reservation in the cycle (only in non-safe-mode)
5. Log the full cycle, scores, and resolution to the evidence ledger

## Dependency
- Requires Track 1 (Decision Core) for the conflict intervention loss matrix
- Operates on reservation data already in TuiSharedState (DbStatSnapshot)
- Reuses fnmatch logic from mcp-agent-mail-guard crate

## Files
- crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/atc.rs (conflict module within ATC)

## Tests
- Unit: no reservations → empty graph, no conflicts
- Unit: two non-overlapping exclusive reservations → no hard edge
- Unit: two overlapping exclusive reservations → hard edge
- Unit: overlapping NON-exclusive reservations → no hard edge (compatible)
- Unit: same pattern in different projects → no conflict (project scoping)
- Unit: A→B→A hard cycle detected as deadlock
- Unit: A→B→C→A three-agent hard cycle detected
- Unit: soft conflict (interest overlap) → advisory, NOT deadlock
- Unit: soft conflict does NOT appear in Tarjan SCC input
- Unit: deadlock resolution scores agents correctly
- Unit: pattern overlap detection (exact, prefix-glob, wildcard)
- Unit: ATC agent excluded from conflict graph
- Unit: advisory rate limiting enforced (no spam)
- Property: Tarjan SCC on hard edges finds all and only true cycles
- Integration: reservation change events trigger graph updates","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:53:26.788488122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:23.570827021Z","source_repo":".","deleted_at":"2026-04-03T01:13:23.570761458Z","deleted_by":"br-rebuild","delete_reason":"rebuild: not in JSONL","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-is0pn","depends_on_id":"br-emhdw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:53:27.086141599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
